channel.title: Brazil Elections 2026
channel.description: Real-time monitoring of the 2026 Brazilian presidential elections.

item.title: Trama golpista: Moraes determina prisão de últimos réus livres do núcleo 3, composto pelos 'kids pretos'
item.description: Supreme Court Justice Alexandre de Moraes ordered the arrest of the remaining free members of the 'kids pretos' group, a cell of special forces military personnel who plotted to assassinate Lula, Alckmin, and Moraes in 2022 to keep Bolsonaro in power after his electoral defeat. Sentences range from 1 year and 11 months to 24 years, with most to be served in closed regime. The First Chamber of the STF unanimously upheld the convictions in November 2023.

item.title: É #FAKE que governo Lula quer taxar entregadores de aplicativo em R$ 10 por corrida
item.description: It is false that the Lula government wants to tax app delivery workers R$10 per trip. The proposal under discussion in Congress establishes a minimum wage floor of R$8.50 to R$10 per delivery, to be paid by platforms to workers—not a charge on them. The Presidency’s Secretariat of Communication confirmed there is no intent to create a new tax on this category.

item.title: Governo deve rejeitar proposta de Trump para Brasil receber estrangeiros capturados nos EUA
item.description: The Lula government should reject the U.S. proposal for Brazil to house foreigners captured in the U.S. in its prisons, along with other demands such as sharing biometric data of refugees and presenting a plan to eliminate the PCC and CV. Brazilian diplomats argue the proposal violates the LGPD, lacks legal basis in Brazilian law, and that Brazil already has structured actions and existing cooperation with the U.S. to combat organized crime.

item.title: Rio: Tribunal de Justiça concede habeas corpus a vereador Salvino Oliveira
item.description: The Rio de Janeiro Court of Justice granted habeas corpus to councilman Salvino Oliveira (PSD), releasing him after his arrest last Wednesday (11). He was targeted in the Red Legacy operation, accused of negotiating with trafficker Edgar Alves de Andrade, known as 'Doca', a leader of the Red Command, to obtain permission for electoral campaigning in the Gardênia Azul community. Oliveira, 28 and in his first term, claimed he was a victim of 'a fight that is not mine'.

item.title: Crusoé: Relação com Brasil “não vai melhorar até eleições”, diz cônsul israelense
item.description: Israel's consul-general in São Paulo, Rafael Erdreich, stated that diplomatic relations between Brazil and Israel are at their lowest possible level and will not improve until the 2026 Brazilian elections. While acknowledging the historical partnership between the two nations, he criticized the Lula government's stance on the Iran war and the Hamas conflict. The remarks were made in an exclusive interview with journalists and reported by Crusoé.

item.title: O que há num sobrenome?
item.description: The article examines FlÃ¡vio Bolsonaro's candidacy as a tool of right-wing control by Jair Bolsonaro, highlighting debates over whether he represents a 'moderate' or reactionary Bolsonarism. Experts discuss if his candidacy reflects moderation or embodies a reactionary tendency linked to goals such as amnesty for coup plotters, impeachment of STF justices, and alignment with Trump, raising concerns for Brazilian democracy.

item.title: Caso sobre quebra de sigilo de Lulinha será analisado por plenário do STF após pedido de Gilmar
item.description: Minister Gilmar Mendes requested a spotlight on the STF's judgment regarding the suspension of the approvals by the INSS Parliamentary Inquiry Committee (CPI), including the banking and fiscal data breach of Lulinha, son of President Lula. The judgment, previously conducted virtually, will now be resumed in the physical plenary session, with a date to be set by STF President Edson Fachin. So far, only Minister Flávio Dino has voted, upholding his suspension of the data breaches, arguing that the 'bloc' voting of 87 requests fails to meet constitutional and legal requirements.

item.title: Cleitinho lidera disputa ao governo de Minas com até 40% das intenções
item.description: Senator Cleitinho Azevedo leads all vote intention scenarios for the Minas Gerais gubernatorial race in 2026, reaching up to 40% in one simulation according to Real Time Big Data. Senator Rodrigo Pacheco consistently ranks second, while Alexandre Kalil and Mateus Simões show strong support. In scenarios excluding Cleitinho, either Pacheco or Kalil take the lead, suggesting a polarized contest if he does not run.

item.title: “Nossa pressão deu certo”, afirma Zema sobre maioria no STF
item.description: Minas Gerais Governor Romeu Zema (Novo) celebrated the Second Chamber of the STF's decision to maintain the preventive detention of banker Daniel Vorcaro, controller of Banco Master, stating 'our pressure worked'. He attributed the outcome to public mobilization around the case.

item.title: Petrobras aumenta preço do diesel após Lula zerar impostos
item.description: Petrobras announced a R$ 0.38 per liter increase in diesel A prices for distributors, effective Saturday, the 14th. The hike came one day after President Lula zeroed PIS and Cofins taxes on diesel nationwide. The move aims to offset federal revenue loss from the tax exemption.

item.title: Lula revoga visto de diplomata do governo Trump que queria visitar Bolsonaro
item.description: President Lula revoked the visa of Darren Beattie, a senior U.S. State Department official for Brazil, who sought to visit imprisoned ex-president Bolsonaro over coup attempt charges. The STF had previously denied the visit, citing concerns of foreign interference in an election year. The visa, originally granted for a critical minerals forum in São Paulo, was canceled due to omitted information and false statements about the visit's true purpose.

item.title: Quaest: 28% dizem que apoio de Trump a Flávio Bolsonaro aumentam chance de votar no senador; 32% dizem que aumentam chance de votar em Lula
item.description: Quaest poll shows 32% of Brazilians believe Donald Trump's support for Flávio Bolsonaro would increase their likelihood of voting for Lula, compared to 28% who say it would boost support for Bolsonaro. The effect is polarized: Trump's backing strengthens Lula among his base (79%) and Bolsonaro among his (80%), while independents and non-religious voters lean toward third-way candidates.

item.title: A reação nos bastidores do governo Lula ao encontro de emissário de Trump com Bolsonaro
item.description: An emissary linked to Donald Trump attempted to visit jailed former president Jair Bolsonaro, initially approved by Supreme Court Minister Alexandre de Moraes but later blocked due to concerns over foreign interference in Brazil’s political process. The Lula administration viewed the move as a politically charged gesture with potential electoral impact and a dangerous precedent for external influence, sparking diplomatic discomfort with Brazil’s Foreign Ministry. Bolsonaro’s hospitalization for bronchopneumonia halted any prison-based political activity, but Lula used the incident to draw parallels with recent restrictions on Brazilian officials entering the U.S., escalating bilateral tensions.

item.title: Governo avalia que assessor de Trump omitiu real motivo de visita e transformou viagem ao Brasil em ato político
item.description: The Lula government assesses that Donald Trump’s advisor on Brazil affairs, Darren Beattie, concealed the real purpose of his visit to Brazil, turning it into a political act by scheduling undeclared meetings, including a request to visit jailed former president Bolsonaro. The revocation of Beattie’s visa was justified on the principle of reciprocity, countering efforts to forge a bolsonarista-trumpista alliance ahead of the 2026 presidential election.

item.title: Lula diz que proibiu assessor do governo Trump de entrar no Brasil
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva barred Darren Beattie, an adviser to the Trump administration, from entering Brazil, citing reciprocity over the continued blocking of visas for Health Minister Alexandre Padilha and his family. The move followed the revocation of Beattie’s visa by Brazil’s Foreign Ministry due to incomplete and false information about the trip’s true purpose—visiting jailed former president Jair Bolsonaro—despite the original justification being attendance at the Brazil-U.S. Critical Minerals Forum in São Paulo.

item.title: 55% desaprovam governo Lula em MG, diz Real Time Big Data
item.description: A Real Time Big Data poll in Minas Gerais shows 55% of voters disapprove of President Lula's government, compared to 42% who approve, with 3% undecided. Qualitatively, 46% rate the administration as 'bad' or 'terrible', while 33% see it as 'excellent' or 'good', and 20% as 'fair'. Conducted March 10–11, 2026, with 2,000 respondents and a 2-point margin of error, the survey reflects negative sentiment in one of Brazil’s key electoral states.

item.title: Paes volta a criticar prisão de ex-secretário, vê uso político da polícia e diz ter recebido admissão de erro
item.description: Rio de Janeiro Mayor Eduardo Paes (PSD) renewed his criticism of the preventive arrest of councilman Salvino Oliveira (PSD), former municipal secretary, calling it a political use of the Civil Police. Paes said he received indications that the arrest was a mistake, without revealing the source, and argued the measure aims to politically benefit Deputy Felipe Curi, an ally of Cláudio Castro (PL).

item.title: Crusoé: Lula não é mais o presidente do Brasil
item.description: President Lula no longer represents the Brazilian state nor governs the country, having decided not to attend the inauguration of José Antonio Kast, Chile’s new president. This absence reflects a missed opportunity for Brazil to project influence in Latin America, leaving a vacuum that other regional actors may fill.

item.title: Lula assume riscos ao retomar a briga com Trump
item.description: Lula took on risks by reigniting his confrontation with Donald Trump, after Trump’s envoy criticized Brazil and linked Minister Alexandre de Moraes to the Magnitsky Act. The escalation occurs within the 2026 election context, as Lula seeks to capitalize on Bolsonaro’s decline but exposes himself to record-low approval ratings for himself and the STF, per Datafolha. The move blends political calculation with institutional risk, given that the 2025 trade war already saw Bolsonarism align with Trump against Brazil.

item.title: Tarcísio encontra cúpula do PSDB e diz que às vezes se sente tucano
item.description: Representatives of the PSDB’s national leadership met with São Paulo Governor Tarcísio de Freitas (Republicans) to reinforce political proximity between the two groups. Although formal endorsement of his re-election has not yet been made, the PSDB views it as a natural path. Tarcísio stated he feels like a tucano in his vision of the country, the world, and public management, praising the legacy of Mário Covas and the party’s strengthening efforts.

item.title: Evento de Lula transforma Flávio em alvo por gestão de hospital durante governo Bolsonaro
item.description: During the inauguration of the new trauma unit at Hospital Federal do Andaraí, President Lula, Rio Mayor Eduardo Paes, and Health Minister Alexandre Padilha criticized Senator Flávio Bolsonaro for his alleged role in the deterioration of hospital management during the Bolsonaro administration, accusing him of appointing directors and controlling contracts that led to the unit's decline. Paes highlighted practices such as keeping the kitchen closed for 12 months due to costly food transport contracts, while Padilha alleged a broader scheme by traditional politicians to weaken federal hospitals in Rio.

item.title: A um ano eleição presidencial, franceses elegem prefeitos em mais de 34 mil cidades
item.description: France is holding municipal elections on March 15 and 22, 2026, across over 34,875 cities and overseas territories to elect mayors and municipal councilors. The system uses gender-parity lists, requires a second round if no list wins over 50% in the first round, and the elected mayor serves as both local executive and state representative, implementing council decisions and overseeing public order, budget, and urban services.

item.title: Paes diz a Lula que população quer Ozempic no SUS
item.description: Rio de Janeiro Mayor Eduardo Paes told President Lula that the public is demanding the inclusion of Ozempic in the SUS, stating he had promised to expand access to the drug during his 2024 election campaign. Paes said he started using the medication, lost 33 kg, and saw health improvements, and that the drug is already being offered at a municipal health unit in Rio. He urged Lula to encourage Health Minister Alexandre Padilha to evaluate including Ozempic in the SUS, with new announcements expected on March 17.

item.title: PSD definiu Ratinho Jr. como candidato a presidente?
item.description: The PSD has officially defined Paraná Governor Ratinho Jr. as its presidential candidate for the 2026 elections, with formal announcement expected in April. This decision rules out supporting other governors, such as Rio Grande do Sul's Eduardo Leite. The party aims to consolidate a center-right candidacy based on successful state-level governance.

item.title: Lula diz querer saber se Trump oferece tratamento de câncer como o Brasil
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva questioned whether Donald Trump could offer Americans the same free access to cancer treatment that Brazil provides through the SUS, noting that in Brazil treatment must begin within 60 days of diagnosis. He cited initiatives like the Farmácia Popular and expanding access to specialists and exams as priorities to reduce wait times in the public health system.

item.title: Pé-de-Meia evita evasão escolar de 1 a cada 4 alunos, diz estudo
item.description: A study by the Center for Evidence in Integral Education found that the Pé-de-Meia program, launched in 2024 by the Lula government, reduced school dropout rates from 26.4% to 19.9% among students from vulnerable families, preventing 1 in 4 youths from leaving high school. The impact was strongest in Ceará (10 percentage point reduction) and weakest in Paraná (4.4 points), but all 27 federal units exceeded the minimum effectiveness threshold of 2.5 points, confirming national efficacy despite not reducing regional inequalities.

item.title: ‘Eu proibi enquanto não liberar o visto do meu ministro’, diz Lula sobre vinda de assessor de Trump
item.description: President Lula stated he barred the entry of Donald Trump's advisor, Darren Beattie, into Brazil until the U.S. releases the visa for his Health Minister, Alexandre Padilha. Beattie had secured permission to visit jailed former president Jair Bolsonaro, but Supreme Court Minister Alexandre de Moraes revoked the authorization after the Itamaraty clarified the visa was issued solely for attending the Brazil-U.S. Critical Minerals Forum, with no mention of meetings with Bolsonaro.

item.title: Por que Lula proibiu o assessor de Trump que queria visitar Bolsonaro de entrar no Brasil
item.description: Brazil's Ministry of Foreign Affairs revoked the visa of Darren Beattie, a senior advisor at the U.S. State Department, after he attempted to visit jailed former president Jair Bolsonaro without disclosing the purpose, which the Itamaraty deemed an omission and potential political interference. Lula defended the move as reciprocation for the Trump administration's revocation of visas for Health Minister Alexandre Padilha's family in August 2025, stating he would not allow Beattie to enter Brazil until those visas were restored.

item.title: Ex-presidente Bolsonaro dá entrada na UTI com broncopneumonia
item.description: Former President Jair Bolsonaro was admitted to the ICU at DF Star Hospital in Brasília due to bilateral bacterial pneumonia, after falling ill in prison. He is serving a 27-year sentence for attempting a coup d'état and has a history of health complications stemming from the 2018 stabbing. His son, Flávio Bolsonaro, a 2026 presidential pre-candidate, criticized the Supreme Court's denial of humanitarian house arrest.

item.title: Bolsonaro está internado com broncopneumonia bacteriana bilateral, diz hospital
item.description: Former President Jair Bolsonaro was admitted to DF Star Hospital in Brasília with bilateral bacterial bronchopneumonia of probable aspirative origin, following high fever, oxygen desaturation, sweating, and chills. He is currently in the ICU receiving intravenous antibiotic therapy and non-invasive clinical support. His son, Senator Flávio Bolsonaro, and his lawyer have requested his transfer to house arrest due to the worsening of his health condition.

item.title: Crusoé: Juntos, mas separados
item.description: The Psol decided to support Lula's re-election bid in 2026 but rejected the PT's proposal to form a party federation, preserving its ideological independence. The move reflects the Psol's self-perception as a niche party committed to its radical identity and wary of programmatic dilution in broad alliances.

item.title: Fazenda regula financiamento com recursos do Fundo Social para áreas afetadas por chuvas
item.description: Finance Minister Fernando Haddad signed an ordinance regulating the release of R$500 million in financing for individuals and businesses affected by extreme rainfall in February and March 2026, particularly in the Zona da Mata Mineira. The funds come from the 2025 financial surplus of the Social Fund and will support working capital, reconstruction, and replacement of damaged machinery and equipment, with credit operations available until July 4, 2026, through Banco do Brasil and Caixa Econômica Federal.

item.title: O impacto político ‘imediato’ provocado pela internação de Jair Bolsonaro
item.description: The hospitalization of Jair Bolsonaro due to bronchopneumonia has reignited the debate over maintaining his imprisonment in a closed regime, with allies and public opinion sectors pushing for a review of the STF's prior denial of house arrest. According to columnist Mauro Paulino, the episode reinforces Bolsonaro’s victimization narrative, as recurring medical complications tend to provoke criticism of the prison treatment applied to him. Paulino argues the decision should be based on a rigorous and impartial medical evaluation, balancing equality before the law with the need for adequate healthcare for a detainee with a history of clinical issues.

item.title: Crusoé: Pré-candidato Eduardo Leite defende cotas para trans
item.description: Rio Grande do Sul governor and PSD presidential pre-candidate Eduardo Leite advocated for specific public service positions reserved for transgender individuals, stating that public administration must reflect societal diversity. He argued that the public sector must mirror the diversity found in society to be truly representative and effective.

item.title: Com impulso da Geração Z, ex-rapper pode ser novo premiê do Nepal
item.description: Balendra Shah, a former rapper and ex-mayor of Kathmandu, led his Rastriya Swatantra Party (RSP) to a landslide victory in Nepal’s parliamentary elections, securing 182 of 275 seats and an absolute majority. Driven by Gen Z-led protests against corruption and economic hardship, the result positions Shah as the likely new prime minister, even defeating former leader K.P. Sharma Oli in his own constituency.

item.title: Gilmar leva decisão de Dino sobre CPI e Lulinha ao plenário físico
item.description: Supreme Court Justice Gilmar Mendes requested a highlight to move the virtual plenary judgment on Justice Flávio Dino’s decision—which annulled the CPMI-INSS’s bulk approval of bank and fiscal secrecy breaches, including that of Fábio Luís Lula da Silva (Lulinha)—to the physical plenary, restarting the proceedings. Dino had ruled that the CPMI’s bloc vote lacked individualized justification, stating that secrecy breaches require judicial-like procedural rigor, including individual case analysis and voting.

item.title: Itamaraty confirma revogação de visto de assessor de Trump
item.description: Brazil's Foreign Ministry revoked the visa of Darren Beattie, a Trump administration official, due to omission and falsification of information regarding his visit to Brazil, where he intended to meet with Jair Bolsonaro. President Lula tied Beattie's potential entry to the release of visas for Health Minister Alexandre Padilha, which were blocked by the U.S. The Supreme Federal Court also denied Bolsonaro's request to receive Beattie, citing lack of diplomatic notification and concerns of foreign interference in an election year.

item.title: Lula chama Bolsonaro de “blefador, mentiroso e agressor”
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva strongly criticized former President Jair Bolsonaro, stating Brazil should never have elected a leader who is a 'bluffer, liar, and aggressor,' who offended women, Black people, overweight individuals, and Indigenous groups. Lula linked the critique to the spread of political disinformation and advocated for a society grounded in decency, dignity, and respect. The remarks were made during the inauguration of new facilities at the Federal Hospital of Andaraí in Rio de Janeiro, attended by Mayor Eduardo Paes and Health Minister Alexandre Padilha.

item.title: Como nova internação de Bolsonaro atrapalha os planos de Flávio e do PL para as eleições
item.description: The hospitalization of Jair Bolsonaro in the ICU due to broncopneumonia postponed a planned meeting with his son Flávio Bolsonaro to define the PL's electoral strategy in São Paulo and Minas Gerais. The meeting was intended to decide on the Senate candidate for SP and to discuss support for Governor Romeu Zema in MG, seen as crucial for bolsonarism. With Bolsonaro isolated and family access restricted, decisions are delayed until his recovery.

item.title: Governo Lula revoga visto de assessor de Trump que queria visitar Bolsonaro
item.description: The Lula government revoked the visa of Darren Beattie, a senior adviser to former U.S. President Donald Trump on Brazil-related policy, barring him from entering Brazil. Lula announced the decision on Friday morning, May 13, and Itamaraty confirmed the revocation. The move underscores diplomatic tensions between Brazil and the U.S., particularly concerning figures associated with former President Jair Bolsonaro.

item.title: A ampla vantagem de Cleitinho sobre nomes de Lula e Zema na corrida ao governo de MG
item.description: Cleitinho Azevedo leads voter intentions for the Minas Gerais government race with a wide margin, reaching 34% in the full-candidate scenario according to Real Time Big Data. He surpasses Rodrigo Pacheco (19%) and Mateus Simões (9%), despite Zema's announced resignation to run for president. Cleitinho's advantage grows in scenarios without Pacheco or Kalil, reaching up to 40%.

item.title: Lula anuncia revogação de visto de assessor de Trump que viria ao Brasil
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva announced the revocation of the U.S. visa for Darren Beattie, a senior adviser on Brazil policy at the U.S. State Department. The measure will remain in effect until Brazilian Health Minister Alexandre Padilha regains permission to enter the United States, as his visa is currently blocked. Lula linked the two decisions, stating he barred Beattie from visiting imprisoned former president Jair Bolsonaro while Padilha’s U.S. visa remains denied.

item.title: Lula monta time de campanha com aliados de longa data
item.description: The PT has defined Lula’s campaign team for his 2026 reelection bid, prioritizing long-time allies. Edinho Silva will serve as general coordinator, Paulo Okamotto will oversee popular committees and social media, Gilberto Carvalho will manage the agenda, and Raul Rabelo will act as the campaign’s media strategist. Additional figures include Monica Valent for U.S. mobilization and José Sergio Gabrielli for the government program.

item.title: Governo Lula revoga visto de assessor de Trump que visitaria Bolsonaro na cadeia
item.description: The Lula administration revoked the diplomatic visa of Darren Beattie, a senior adviser at the U.S. Department of State, who planned to visit jailed former president Jair Bolsonaro. The move followed findings that Beattie misrepresented the purpose of his trip, initially stating he would attend a critical minerals forum but later seeking a meeting with Bolsonaro. Lula framed the decision as reciprocal, citing the prior denial of visas to Brazilian officials, including Health Minister Alexandre Padilha and his family, for the UN General Assembly in New York.

item.title: Pré-candidatos do PSD apoiarão o escolhido pelo partido
item.description: Pré-candidatos do PSD apoiarão o escolhido pelo partido

item.title: Petrobras anuncia aumento do diesel um dia após o governo zerar PIS/Cofins
item.description: Petrobras raised the price of diesel A by 38 cents per liter effective Saturday, March 14, 2026, one day after the federal government zeroed PIS/Cofins taxes on diesel. The company states the impact on consumers will be mitigated by government measures, including tax exemption (32 cents/liter) and a subsidy program (30 cents/liter), totaling 62 cents of expected compensation. The increase occurs amid international oil volatility driven by the escalating conflict between the U.S., Israel, and Iran, pushing global fuel prices upward.

item.title: Instagram testa links clicáveis em legendas e muda o caminho entre conteúdo e conversão
item.description: Instagram is testing clickable links directly in post and Reel captions for Meta Verified subscribers, allowing users to access external sites without leaving the post. This feature shortens the path between content and user action, improving conversion potential for business and creator accounts. Currently in gradual rollout, there is no confirmed timeline for broader availability.

item.title: Membro do MPF não pode 'curtir' e compartilhar foto de candidato
item.description: The federal corregedor of the Federal Public Ministry (MPF), Elton Ghersel, issued a recommendation to federal prosecutors prohibiting them from expressing opinions that directly or indirectly support or oppose candidacies, parties, or political projects, under penalty of violating functional duties.

item.title: Pré-campanha de Flávio registra aumento de ataques, mas senador deve 'jogar parado'
item.description: Flávio Bolsonaro's pre-campaign detected an increase in attacks against the senator on social media in recent weeks, coinciding with his rise in electoral polls. The campaign's strategy is to refrain from engaging, maintaining a 'lay low' posture to avoid direct confrontation with President Lula and his allies. The team prioritizes stability and reputational risk containment amid escalating online hostility.

item.title: Governo Lula cancela o visto de Darren Beattie, assessor de Trump
item.description: The Lula government revoked the visa of Darren Beattie, a Donald Trump adviser, preventing his visit to Brazil to meet with imprisoned former president Jair Bolsonaro. The move is a retaliatory measure against U.S. sanctions on Health Minister Alexandre Padilha and his family, whose U.S. entry visas have been suspended since August 2025. Justice Alexandre de Moraes confirmed the visa denial was due to the undisclosed nature of the meeting with Bolsonaro, which falls outside the original diplomatic purpose of the visa.
